description: Final Triangle Assessment Set. All 15 dimensions covered with a minimum of 2 measurements each across Gain and Loss frames. Fixes applied: T05 prompt clarified, T07 vertex B remapped to correctly measure experimentation_risk_tolerance, T01 restored for helping_others_impact gain frame, T13 added for autonomy loss frame and comfort_ambiguous_ownership second measurement.
scoring_note: All triangles use standard drag-toward mechanics. For gain-framed triangles, proximity to a vertex indicates affinity with that dimension. For loss-framed triangles, proximity to a vertex indicates that the absence of that dimension is most painful — which maps to a high score on that dimension. Formula: score = (barycentric_coordinate × 4) + 1, mapping [0,1] to [1,5]. Aggregate multiple measurements per dimension by simple average.

triangles.title: The Meaning of a Win
triangles.prompt: You just hit a major milestone. Drag the ball toward the flavor of satisfaction you feel most strongly.

a.label: The tangible, positive change in the lives of the people you served

b.label: The quiet pride of doing something technically difficult that few others could replicate

c.label: The energy of being recognized as the person who made it happen

triangles.tension_note: Three distinct sources of post-achievement satisfaction: impact on others, internal depth, and external recognition. Mutually exclusive at extremes. Provides gain-frame measurement for helping_others_impact, which otherwise only appears in loss frame.

triangles.title: The Ideal Offer
triangles.prompt: You have three job offers, each perfect in only one way. Drag the ball to the compromise you'd actually make.

a.label: Top-tier salary, but the work is stressful and constant

b.label: Complete flexibility and peace of mind, but the pay is just okay

c.label: A deep sense of community and shared values, but the hours are long

triangles.tension_note: Classic life trade-off between money, time, and community fit. Each vertex imposes a real cost, ensuring the placement reveals genuine priority rather than wishful thinking.

triangles.title: The Nature of the Work
triangles.prompt: If you could design your daily workflow from scratch, which pull is strongest?

a.label: Total independence: you decide the what and the how

b.label: Absolute stability: you know exactly what is expected and your future is secure

c.label: Pure creativity: the chance to build something original and expressive

triangles.tension_note: Three environmental needs that genuinely compete. High autonomy often means high uncertainty, conflicting with security. High security often means prescribed output, conflicting with creativity. Creativity without autonomy is constrained expression.

triangles.title: When the Fog Rolls In
triangles.prompt: You are leading a project where the tools and goals change every week. What keeps you moving?

a.label: The high of conquering a problem that seems impossible to others

b.label: The ability to stay calm and effective even when you don't have all the facts

c.label: The speed at which you can learn new systems and pivot your approach

triangles.tension_note: Three distinct responses to sustained difficulty. The achiever is energised by the obstacle. The resilient person maintains psychological stability in fog. The adapter focuses on acquiring new capability. Mixing value and traits is intentional — the three archetypes are genuinely distinct regardless of category.

triangles.title: The Energy Drain
triangles.prompt: Which of these work environments would burn you out the fastest? Drag the ball toward your biggest dealbreaker.

a.label: Total isolation — no human connection of any kind

b.label: Zero real-world impact — your work changes nothing for anyone

c.label: A completely rigid system — zero creative input ever allowed

triangles.tension_note: Loss-framed measurement for belonging, helping impact, and creativity. Proximity to a vertex means that absence is the most painful — mapping correctly to a high score on that dimension. Prompt fixed to use standard drag-toward mechanics with no contradictory instructions.

triangles.title: The Dealbreaker
triangles.prompt: A company is recruiting you hard. Which single missing element kills the deal?

a.label: No room for growth — you'll be doing the same thing in five years

b.label: No freedom to try things — every decision needs approval before you act

c.label: No real collaboration — it is every person for themselves

triangles.tension_note: Vertex B fixed: original label ('no skin in the game') measured financial motivation, not experimentation tolerance. New label correctly maps to someone high on experimentation who would find required approval for every action genuinely draining.

triangles.title: The Future Self
triangles.prompt: Ten years from now, you are successful. What is the core of that success?

a.label: You are the recognized authority that everyone in the field knows by name

b.label: You have mastered a craft to a depth that very few humans reach

c.label: You are the glue — the person who builds the teams that change the world

triangles.tension_note: Purified version: recognition and achievement cleanly separated. Recognition faces outward (reputation), mastery faces inward (depth), social glue faces relational (team impact). Three genuinely distinct archetypes of success.

triangles.title: Conquest vs. Status
triangles.prompt: In a high-stakes project, you can only protect one thing. Which is it?

a.label: The internal win: knowing you conquered a challenge at the edge of your ability

b.label: The external win: being seen as the definitive expert and leader in the room

c.label: The steady win: knowing your work provides a predictable foundation for your future

triangles.tension_note: Best triangle in the set. Cleanly separates internal achievement (Conqueror), external reputation (Authority), and predictability (Strategist). Loss framing — protect one — forces honest prioritisation.

triangles.title: The Orchestrator vs. The Maker
triangles.prompt: A new tool lets you produce ten times more work, but the nature of work changes. Where do you find your flow?

a.label: Directing a system of agents and tools to achieve a massive scaled result

b.label: Doing the core creative work yourself because you love the process

c.label: Rapid iteration — trying fifty small experiments to find what works

triangles.tension_note: AI-era behavioral triangle. Orchestration vs personal creation vs rapid iteration maps onto three genuinely different ways people will relate to AI-augmented work. The prompt grounds the tension in a concrete and relevant scenario.

triangles.title: When the Plan Falls Apart
triangles.prompt: A project you have worked on for months is suddenly cancelled. What is your immediate, natural response?

a.label: You immediately start scanning for the next wave to ride, unbothered by the loss

b.label: You stay calm and start methodically organizing the chaos into a new structure

c.label: You focus on the team, making sure everyone feels supported through the transition

triangles.tension_note: Loss-framed trait triangle. Three distinct responses to disruption: the Agile Adapter (moves on fast), the Stabilizer (creates order in chaos), and the Social Glue (holds the people together). Provides second loss-frame measurement for adaptability, resilience, and collaboration.

triangles.title: The Invisible Constraints
triangles.prompt: You land a role with great pay and interesting work. Which hidden constraint would quietly erode your motivation over time?

a.label: Every process is set — you execute what others designed, with no say in how it works

b.label: The output is always a group product — your individual contribution is never visible or credited

c.label: The income is unpredictable — some months are great, others leave you anxious

triangles.tension_note: New triangle added to provide: (1) loss-frame second measurement for autonomy_independence, which previously had only one gain-frame reading; (2) second measurement for comfort_ambiguous_ownership, which previously appeared only in T11; (3) loss-frame second measurement for financial_success, which previously had only one gain-frame reading. The slow-erosion framing surfaces values that operate as background needs rather than conscious priorities.
